scenes.phrase: You've paid Spotify more money every single year since 2023
And artists are getting paid less per stream than ever
Let me say that again — most insane sentence in the music industry
Paying more for Spotify today than any point in 18-year history
2023. 2024. 2025. 2026. Four price hikes in four years.
Same app, same songs, same skip button. Price goes up anyway.
Artists watching their per-stream payout collapse
You pay more, they get less. Spotify keeps the difference.
If that sounds like a scam, it is. Very polite. Green logo.

cmds.text: if that sounds like a scam...

scenes.phrase: We are going to read the receipt. All of it.

cmds.text: nobody at Spotify wants you to do this.

scenes.phrase: Where money goes. Why artists quit. App worse. Podcasts. Fake numbers. Weapons.
Not a hit piece. This is an audit. Where did your $11.99 go?
2021. $9.99 since 2014. Seven years. Zero increases.
$10 for every song ever recorded. Best deal in history.
June 2023. First hike. $10.99. Market conditions.
Real reason: ran out of new subscribers. Wall Street demanded growth.
April 2024. Second hike. $11.99. Expansion of features.
Audiobooks. 15 hrs/mo. You didn't ask. Never used. Added anyway.
That's not a feature. That's a hostage.
July 2025. Third hike. Individual $12.99. Family $19.99. Duo $17.99.
2026. Fourth hike. $14.99. 50% more than 3 years ago.
